title: Real-trajectory ingestion and artifact-staging manifest (v1)
description: Dataset-agnostic, bring-your-own-dataset contract for staging real pedestrian/agent trajectory data. The repository never commits or hosts raw external data; this manifest records only metadata, license acknowledgment, retrieval instructions, the canonical conversion shape, checksums, split naming, and the durable pointer format. Mirrors the External artifact pointer category in docs/context/artifact_evidence_vocabulary.md. Schema rules here are structural; semantic gates (license acknowledgment, gitignored staging, fail-closed eligibility) are enforced by robot_sf.data_ingestion.real_trajectory_contract.run_preflight.

dataset_id.description: Stable, filesystem-safe dataset identifier, e.g. 'eth_hotel' or 'my_lab_recordings'.

url.description: Upstream project/source URL, or null for purely local recordings.

version.description: Dataset version or release tag chosen by the supplier.

citation.description: Citation or attribution string for the source dataset.

access_date.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the supplier obtained the data.

name.description: License name as understood by the supplier, e.g. 'CC-BY-NC-SA-3.0' or 'proprietary-internal'.

posture.description: 'bring-your-own': supplier holds the rights and stages locally (default). 'project-hosted' requires an explicit maintainer decision.

supplier_acknowledgment.description: Supplier asserts they hold the rights to use this data under the named license. Must be true for the bring-your-own posture (enforced by preflight).

redistribution.description: The repository never redistributes raw external data. Always false.

instructions.description: Reproducible steps another machine follows to obtain an equivalent local copy.

download_url.description: Optional direct download URL. null when access is license-gated or manual.

fail_closed.description: When true, dependent pipelines must fail with an actionable error if the local copy is missing rather than silently substituting a proxy.

algorithm.description: Checksum algorithm for the aggregate staged-file tree hash.

tree_sha256.description: Aggregate hash over staged files, filled on real staging. 'pending'/null before staging.

expected_tree_sha256.description: Pinned expected hash to detect drift after first trusted staging.

frame_rate_hz.description: Sampling rate of the canonical converted frames in Hz.

length_unit.description: Spatial unit of position fields in the source data.

timestamp_field.description: Source column/key holding the per-row timestamp or frame index.

agent_id_field.description: Source column/key holding the per-agent identifier.

position_fields.description: Ordered source columns/keys for agent position, e.g. ['x', 'y'].

map_context_field.description: Source field or asset id identifying scene/map context, or null when not available.

missing_data_behavior.description: How to treat gaps/missing rows during conversion.

naming.description: Split-naming convention used by the supplier.

members.description: Named splits, e.g. ['train', 'val', 'test'] or scene names.

staging_dir.description: Git-ignored local staging directory for raw/converted files. Must resolve under output/ or the ROBOT_SF_EXTERNAL_DATA_ROOT external root (enforced by preflight).

local_only_raw.description: Raw external files stay local and are never committed. Always true.

durable_storage_target.description: Durable pointer for project-generated derived artifacts, e.g. a 'wandb://...' artifact URI or 'local-only-byo' when only the manifest/checksum is tracked.

pii_reviewed.description: Supplier confirms a privacy/PII review was performed for the data.

notes.description: Privacy/licensing notes and any anonymization applied.

availability.description: Local availability state. 'missing' until a copy is staged; 'validated' only after checksum verification.

benchmark_eligibility.description: Downstream evidence tier. Must stay below 'benchmark_candidate' until availability is 'validated' (enforced by preflight).
